def: s.m. pl. The five Pānḍav princes.
s.m. Learned man, scholar; teacher, schoolmaster; a title of Brāhmaṇs (cf. pāṅḍā, panḍā).
adj. = pandrah-wāṅ, q.v.
s.m. = pandarwāṛā, q.v.
v.t. To coax, wheedle, &c. = phandlānā, q.v.
s.f. The fifteenth day of a month.

adj. & s.m. Learned, wise; shrewd, skilful, clever, educated; — scholar, learned or wise man, learned Brāhmaṇ; teacher, master, professor, doctor, philosopher, Pandit; a title of respect to Hindūs who are learned in the Brahmanical theology
subs: s.m. One who esteems himself a scholar, an ignorant pedant.

def: s.f. The learning, or the status, of a panḍit; learning, scholarship, knowledge, wisdom; pedantry, display of learning
subs: v.n. To make a display of learning.

def: s.f. = panḍitānī, q.v.
s.f. = panḍitānī, q.v.
s.f. The wife of a panḍit; teacher, school-mistress.
s.f. = S پانڐتيه पाण्डित्य pānḍitya, s.m. Scholarship, erudition, learning, wisdom, prudence.
s.m. Prison, gaol; — a gambling house.
s.m. = H پانڐتي पाण्डिती pānḍitī, s.f. Scholarship, erudition, learning, wisdom, prudence.
s.f. The wife of a panḍā or priest; — the wife of a pānḍe.
s.m. A species of sugar-cane (cf. pauṅḍā).
= H پندر पंदर pandar, Corr. of pandrah, q.v. (used in comp.)
adj. Fifteen.
adj. (f. wīṅ), Fifteenth; — pandrah-wīṅ, s.f. The fifteenth day of the month; the fifteenth day of a half month, the full or the new moon.

adj. & s.m. Yellowish white, white, pale; pale or yellowish white colour; light-coloured soil, a mixture of clay and sand; land dependent on rain-water; — name of a sovereign of Hastināpur (ancient Dehli), and nominal father of Yudhishṭhir and the other four Pānḍav princes who are distinguished in the great wars related in the Mahābhārat; — s.f. The plant Glycine debilis (com. called mashāni)
subs: s.m.f. Light-coloured soil; a country with a light-coloured soil
pānḍu-bhūm, s.f. The land of the Pānḍavs, the city of Hastināpur
s.m. A son of Pānḍu, an epithet of any one of the Pānḍav princes
s.m. White woollen covering or blanket; a kind of stone (limestone or marble)
s.f. The housings of a royal elephant; a carriage covered with a sort of blanket. — pānḍu-lomā, s.f. The plant Glycine debilis
s.f. Pale or light-coloured soil, chalky soil; — the opal; — pānḍu-nāg, s.m. The plant Rottleria tinctoria

def: adj. & s.m. = پانڐ pānḍu, q.v.
s.m. Name of a sovereign of ancient Dehli, &c. = pānḍu, q.v.
s.m. Ripe fruit, fruit; a berry.
s.f. See s.v. pan, 'water.'
adj. Relating to the Pānḍavas or children of the Pānḍus (see next).
adj. & s.m. Yellowish-white, &c. ( = pānḍar); — a form of jaundice; white leprosy, vitiligo
subs: s.m. lit. 'The pale tree'; the plant Wrightia antidysenterica.

def: s.f. A species of hawk or falcon.
s.m. Light-coloured or pale soil, mixed soil of clay and sand.
s.f. A patch or parcel of fruit or flower trees for transplantation; — a young flowering shrub or plant.
s.m. = pāṅk, q.v.
s.m. Weaver's woof-frame.
s.m. Sea-salt, culinary salt obtained from sea-sand.
adj. Crooked, deformed, &c., see pangu.
adj. (f. -ī), Watery, thin, weak; delicate, tender.
s.m. A species of fish (see next).

s.f. Line, row, &c. = pankti and pāṅt, qq.v.; a row of people sitting down at a meal; a company where seats are allotted according to rank; society, fellowship, brotherhood; body, band, company; tribe, caste
s.m. See s.v. pan, 'water.'
s.f. = paṅgat, q.v.
s.f. See s.v. pan, 'water.'
s.m. The coral tree, Erythrina indica.
s.f. = pangat, q.v.
adj. See پنگو paṅgu.
adj. & s.m. (f. pangvī). Lame, crippled, halt, deformed, crooked-legged, bandy-legged; — One who has lost his legs; a cripple; a bandy-legged person.
adj. = pangu, and panglā, q.v.; — s.m. A horse of a glossy or silvery-white colour; — also = paṅgutā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Lame, deformed, &c. = paṅgu, q.v.
s.f. = S پنگتو पंगुत्व paṅgutva, s.m. Lameness; deformity, crookedness, bandy-leggedness.
s.m. = S پنگتا पंगुता pangutā, s.f. Lameness; deformity, crookedness, bandy-leggedness.
adj. Wide, broad, spacious.
s.m. = panhī, q.v.
s.m. Money paid for a clue to lost property, &c.; the person to whom money is paid for the clue.
v.t. = panānā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of reed.
s.m. See s.v. pan, 'water.'
s.m. See s.v. pan, 'water.'
s.m. See s.v. paṇ, 'Wager,' &c.
s.f. Slipper, sandal, shoe, boot.
s.f. = panhī, q.v.
adj. & s.f. Afflicted with the fluor albus; one so afflicted.
s.m. = panhārī, q.v.s.v. pan, 'water.'
s.m.f. The hand
subs: s.m. The glomerous fig-tree, Ficus glomerata
pāṇi-grahaṇ, s.m. Laying hold of the hand, marriage (the junction of the hands of the bride and bridegroom forming part of the ceremony)
s.f. lit. 'Taken by the hand'; a bride wedded according to the ritual
adv. With the hands put flat together (as in supplication).

def: s.m. Water (syn. jal); rain-water, rain ( = meṅh); semen, sperm; water or lustre (of a gem, &c.); lustre, sparkle, polish, brightness, beauty; spirit, mettle, blood, breed; character, reputation, honour; chastity, modesty, delicacy, sense of shame
subs: (-meṅ), To have a cataract (in the eye); to have a swollen scrotum, to suffer from hydrocele
v.n. To disinfect water by plunging heated iron into it
(meṅ), To fill vessels with water, to draw or fetch water; to be humiliated or ashamed; to feel a sense of inferiority (before or in the presence of); to confess inferiority
v.n. To perspire with shame; to be overwhelmed with shame
v.n. Water to run over or inundate (a field, &c.); (fig.) to be wasted, ruined, or destroyed
(-par), To impart lustre (to), to brighten, polish, gild; to plunge into, or submerge in, water; to dissipate, waste, destroy
(or pī-pī-ke) duʻā denā (-ko), To pray for one excessively (i.e. till the throat, becoming dry, must be moistened by drinking)
(or pī-pī-ke) kosnā (-ko), To curse excessively, to heap curses (upon)
pānī ćalnā, v.n. To shed tears; to be afflicted with the fluor albus; to be disgraced
(meṅ), v.n. To drop water; to drop water into the mouth of a dying person
v.n. To absorb water (a wound); to be pregnant
v.n. To give out water; to sweat
(-ko), To show water (to); to bring (a horse, &c.) to water, to water (a horse, &c.)
(-ko), To give water (to), to water (plants, &c.), to irrigate; to offer a libation of water to the manes of a deceased person aftor the corpse has been burnt
s.m. One whose duty it is to offer a libation of water to a deceased person; a son
adj. Thinner than water; watery; cheap, insignificant, worthless; easy
To make or render easy, to facilitate; to put to shame, to abash; to disgrace; to defeat
v.n. To make a bridge before there is water (to bridge); to devise plans against an improbable contingency
s.m. lit. 'Bubble of water'; anything unstable, weak or frail
v.n. To turn off water into another channel; to go or swim against the stream, to breast or stem the waves
v.n. To let out water, to make an outlet for water
adj. & s.m. Aquatic; an aquatic animal
v.n. To make water; — v.t. To convert into water, to liquefy, melt; to make easy, to facilitate; to abash; to dishearten
s.f. A bag or mass of water; anything very watery (as rice, herbs, &c.)
v.t. To give to the flood, to throw away, waste, squander; to sell very cheap or at a sacrifice
v.n. Water (of a place) to affect (one) injuriously; water to be unwholesome (e.g. pānī yahāṅ lagtā hai, 'The water here is unwholesome)
v.n. To draw water (a ship; = pānī toṛnā)
v.n. To run or flow (into); to leak; to be absorbed, be evaporated, to dry up; — to exhibit signs confirming a suspicion; to look suspicious
v.n. To revive a forgotten quarrel; to sow dissension, to set at loggerheads
v.n. To leak, drop, drip, ooze; semen to be discharged
v.n. To die instantly or suddenly (i.e. without having time to ask for water to cool one's dying lips); to be slain with a single stroke of a sword, &c.
To melt away, dissolve, liquefy; to melt, fail utterly (the heart); to become water, become watery or thin; to become loose or flaccid; to become cold as water; — to be very easy or simple.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi پانی (pānī), from Apabhramsa पाणिउ (pāṇiu), from Prakrit पाणीअ (pāṇīa), from Sanskrit पानीय (pānīya, “water, drink”).

def: s.m. Incorr. for پان pāṇi, q.v.
adj. Under a vow; resolute (see pranī).
s.m. = pāʼě-ća; pāʼīza, qq.v.
s.m. Anything living in water, &c. ( = paniyā, q.v.).
adj. (f. -ī), Watery, thin; insipid, tasteless ( = pan-sā).
s.m. Name of the most eminent Hindu grammarian (regarded as an inspired Muni).
adj. Relating to the grammarian Pāṇini; written or composed by Pāṇini.
s.m. Cheese
subs: (-ko), To flatter and please another to one's own advantage

def: s.f. A patch or parcel of fruit or flower trees for transplantation; — a young flowering shrub or plant.
adj. Made of cheese, cheesy; — adv. After the manner of manufacturing cheese
subs: (or panīrī-kā) jamānā, v.n. To mention a circumstance or thing in which one is interested in a casual or off-hand manner in the course of conversation; not to come straight to the point; — to make out a claim.

def: adj. To be drunk; fit, proper, or necessary to be drunk; drinkable
subs: (pānīya+ām˚), s.m. A kind of fruit, Flacourtia cataphracta
s.m. The aquatic plant Pistia stratiotes
s.m. The plant Vernonia (or Serratula) anthelminthica

def: s.m. Water ( = pānī)
subs: s.m. Scarcity of water; famine caused by excess of rain ( = pan-kāl).

def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to water; living in water; watery; aquatic ( = pānī-kā); — anything that lives in water; a water-snake; a water-fowl, the purple coot, Porphyrio poliocephalus ( = pan-kavvā, q.v.).
s.m. A kind of fruit, Flacourtia cataphracta.
v.t. To water, irrigate; — v.n. To yield water, to throw off water (as milk in coagulating); to drip, trickle, ooze; to water (as the tongue); to become thin and watery.
s.m. = paniyālā, q.v.
adj. Watered, flooded, inundated.
s.m. = paniyālā, q.v.
adj. Five; (in gaming) cinque; (in horse-dealer's idiom) seven years old
subs: s.m. 'Five waters or rivers'; the land of five rivers, the Panjāb (situated to the north-west of Hindūstān)
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to the Panjāb; — an inhabitant or a native of the Panjāb; — s.f. The language spoken in the Panjāb
s.f. The five chapters of the Qorʼān which are read during the period of mourning of a Musalmān
s.m. The five holy persons, viz. Mohammad; 'Alī; Fātimah; Hasan; Husain
s.m. Five years; a five-year old (horse)
adj. Of five years, quinquennial; five years old
s.m. Five sūras or chapters of the Qorʼān; a stone on which five sūras of the Qorʼān are engraved (and which is used as a charm)
s.m. A sort of link or torch with five branches for lights ( = panja; panjī)

s.m. The five vices (of a horse); a very vicious horse; — adj. Very vicious; — panj ʻaib-i-sharaʻī, s.m. The five vices or sins, viz. robbery; adultery; gaming; drunkenness; falsehood; — adj. & s.m. Very vicious; — one who is very vicious, one who is addicted to the five vices
adj. & s.m. Five-fold; — the prayers appointed for five periods of the day
s.m. The five senses; the five stated daily prayers ( = panj-gāna); any collection of five things
panj-gosha, adj. Five-fold, quintuple; five cornered, pentangular
adj. Turned five, or ten years old (a horse)
adj. Under five years old (a horse).

def: s.m. An aggregate of five (e.g. tīn panje pandrah, 'three fives are fifteen'); — also = panja, q.v.

s.m. An aggregate of five; the five of a suit of cards, or the five on a die; a cast or turn up of five with dice; — the hand with the fingers extended; claw, paw (of a tiger, &c.); clutch, grasp, possession, power; the five-fingered instrument of the religious mendicant; a sort of link or torch resembling the five fingers, or having five branches ( = panj-shāḵẖa); a hand made of ivory (to scratch the back with); the fore part of a foot or shoe; — cinq-foil
subs: (-kā), To twist or turn round the hand of an adversary by interlocking fingers with him; (fig.) to overcome, overpower
(-se), To interlock fingers with an adversary and endeavour to twist his hand or wrist
s.m. An iron instrument resembling a hand with which wrestlers exercise themselves by locking their fingers into those of the instrument; — a kind of bread bearing the marks of five fingers
(-se), To overcome an antagonist by twisting his hand or wrist, &c. ( = panja phernā); to carry off the palm
v.n. To walk on tip-toe; to walk on stilts, to strut
v.t. To claw; to snap or snatch at; — panja-i-maryam, s.f. A grass resembling a pair of claws interlocked
v.t. To get hold of, get into one's clutches; to bring under subjection

def: s.m. See s.v. panj for all compounds of which that word is the first member.
adj. Fifty.
adj. Fiftieth.
s.f. A door-frame.
s.m. The ribs, the side; side or quarter (syn. t̤araf).
s.m. A cage; a skeleton; the ribs, the thorax; the human frame, the body; a rib
subs: v.n. To become a skeleton, to be emaciated, become thin or lean
v.n. The bones and joints of the frame to become loose.

def: s.f. A rib ( = panjar).
s.m. One who joins or solders, one whose business it is to solder pots and pans, &c.
s.f. A sort of link or torch with five branches for lights ( = panj-shāḵẖa; panja).
s.f. A calendar, an almanac; a journal; account-book; the register or record of human actions kept by Yama (the judge of the dead)
subs: s.m. An almanac maker; an astronomer.

def: s.f. See s.v. pan, 'five.'
v.t. To close or fill in (a hole, — esp. in metal); to solder (syn. jhālnā).
s.f. The number five in the game of ćausar.
adj. Fifth.
s.m. Mud, mire; mud and sand left by inundations, alluvial deposit; slough, bog, quagmire.
s.m. = paṅk, q.v.
s.m. Mud, mire, clay, dirt; slough, quagmire
subs: s.f. 'Hell of mire,' one of the seven divisions of hell
pankaj, s.m. 'Mud-born'; any species of the lotos; — panka-jī, s.f. An epithet of Durgā
s.m. Name of one of the divisions of hell
s.m. 'Mud-growing'; a lotus; — the Indian crane
s.f. 'Mud-shell'; a species of mussel or cockle
s.m. The marine monster called makara or magar; an alligator
s.m. A small fish, Macrognathus pancalus
s.m. A lotos; a water-lily; a crane ( = panka-ruh).

def: s.m. See s.v. panka.
s.m. The aquatic plants Blyxa octandra or vallisneria, and Trapa bispinosa; — a mound, embankment, dam, dike.
adj. (f. -ī), Muddy, miry, clayey, dirty, &c.
s.m. See s.v. panka.
s.m. Feather; wing, pinion; paṅkh-wālā, adj. (f. -ī), Winged, feathered; finned.
s.m. A fan; ventilator.
adj. = paṅkhiyā.
s.f. A kind of woollen cloth which comes from the hilly countries.
s.f. A small fan, a fan.
s.f. A small fan (dim. of paṅkhā).
s.m. A kind of faqīr who fans everybody.
adj. & s.m. Impure, immoral, wicked; perverse, obstinate; — an immoral or wicked man, &c.

ety: paṅkh, q.v.+dim. aff. rī or or ṛī
def: s.f. Petal, leaf of a flower ( = pakhṛī).

ety: paṅkh, q.v.+dim. aff. rī or or ṛī
def: s.f. Petal, leaf of a flower ( = pakhṛī).
s.f. = paṅkhṛī, q.v.
adj. Linear; in or by, line or row.
s.f. A row, line, or set of five; line, range, row, series ( = pāṅt, q.v.); group, flock, troop, multitude, company; (in comp., as first member) ten
subs: s.m. (f. -ī or -ā), lit. 'Going in a line'; an osprey
s.m. lit. 'Having ten chariots,' a name of king Daśa-rath.

def: s.m. A beverage; tamarind-juice or the juice of other sharp fruits made into sherbet.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀡𑁆𑀡 (paṇṇa), from Sanskrit पर्ण (parṇa).

def: s.m. A leaf; (page of a book); gold or silver leaf, foil; a sheet or plate of metal.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀡𑁆𑀡 (paṇṇa), from Sanskrit पर्ण (parṇa).

def: s.m. An emerald.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀡𑁆𑀡 (paṇṇa), from Sanskrit पर्ण (parṇa).

def: s.m. The upper part of a shoe.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀡𑁆𑀡 (paṇṇa), from Sanskrit पर्ण (parṇa).

def: s.m. A reptile, snake, serpent; a serpent-shaped demon; — an emerald (cf. pannā).
s.f. A leaf of metal; foil (of a jewel), tin-foil; tinsel; — a long grass used for thatching.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Paṭhāns.
s.m. Land watered after ploughing.
s.m. = pāṅw, q.v.; — also (for pāṅwoṅ) 'feet.'
s.m. = pāp, q.v.; — agony, anguish, torture.
v.t. To cause to thrive or flourish, to refresh, revive; to promote the prosperity of another. (Rare.)
adj. & s.m. = pāpī, q.v.
s.m. Learned man, scholar; teacher, schoolmaster; a title of Brāhmaṇs (cf. pāṅḍā, panḍā).
s.f. Betel-plant, betel-creeper.
s.m. = pāsā, q.v.
adj. See s.v. pan, 'water.'
s.m. See s.v. pan, 'water.'
s.f. The business of a pansārī or druggist, &c.; dealing in drugs, spices, &c.
s.f. The business of a pansārī or druggist, &c.; dealing in drugs, spices, &c.
s.m. See next.

ety: S. पणस+कारिन्; or पण्य+सार, rt. सृ+इन्
def: s.m. A vendor of drugs, spices, herbs, groceries, &c.; a druggist, spicer, grocer, &c.; (in comp. the word is contracted to pansār, e.g.) — pansār-haṭṭā, s.m. Druggists' quarter, locality where there are many pansārī's shops.
s.f. See s.v. pan, 'five.'
s.f. = pāsī, 'net,' q.v.
v.t. To dung, to manure.
s.f. A kind of small boat, a skiff.
s.m. Dust, &c. = pāṅsu, pāṅs, q.v.
subs: (?), s.m. A kind of vegetable, Chenopodium album
pāṅśuj; pāṇsu-ja, pāṅsuj, s.m. lit. 'Earth-born'; a kind of salt extracted from soil, rock or fossil salt.

def: s.m. Dust; particle of dust; dung, manure; dunghill
subs: v.n. To become dust; to rot; to become manure; to become mildewed; to become mellow (land).

def: s.f. Rib; rib-bones, the side (syn. paslī).
s.m. Sulphuret of iron.
s.f. The fragrant plant Pandanus odoratissimus.
adj. & s.m. Dusty, covered with dust; sullied, defiled, disgraced; — a wicked or profligate man; a species of tree, Cœsalpinia bonducella.
s.m. = panth, q.v.
s.m. Desert field; desert place; a dangerous place.
s.m. A kind of sweetmeat.
s.m. Traveller; passenger.
s.m. A path, way, road, course; a path in morals or religion; doctrine; sect, religious order; calling, vocation, profession, trade, &c.
subs: s.f. The special and peculiar doctrine or teaching of a religious sect.

def: s.m. A path, way, road, course; a path in morals or religion; doctrine; sect, religious order; calling, vocation, profession, trade, &c.
subs: s.f. The special and peculiar doctrine or teaching of a religious sect.

def: s.m. One of the divisions of hell.
s.m. A traveller, wayfarer; the observer of any particular custom or class of opinions; an adherent or follower of any sect or party in religious matters.
s.f. Line, row, string, rank or row (of soldiers), series, range
subs: pāṅtu-pāṅtu, adv. In rows, in lines.

def: s.f. = pāṅti, pāṅt, q.v.
s.m. Foot; leg; foot-print; basis, foundation, root; footing, foothold; part, lot, portion; interference, meddling; steadiness, decision (istiqlāl); responsibility, liability
subs: v.n. The foot to be dislocated
pāṅw uṭhākar ćalnā, v.n. To go or walk quickly, to step out
(-kā), To move (one) from his place, intention, or resolution
v.n. To lose one's footing, be carried off one's legs; to run away, take to flight; to be routed
v.n. The foot to slip; to lose one's balance; to go wrong or astray
(-meṅ), To take the lead, go ahead; to overstep, exceed bounds; to desist from one's former courses
v.n. To be with child, be pregnant
v.n. To suffer numbness or heaviness of the feet
v.n. To have a finger (in); to have a share (in); to be responsible (for)
adv. Afoot, on foot, walking; — pāṅw-pāṅw phirnā, pāṅw-pāṅw ćalnā, pāṅw-pāṅw ḍolnā, v.n. To walk, to toddle (a child)
v.n. To walk in the steps (of another); to imitate or adopt the conduct (of another); to place one leg over the other, to sit cross-legged, to sit at ease
pāṅw-par tesha mār-lenā = apne pānw-meṅ āp kulhārī mārnā
v.n. Of oneself to spoil one's own affair; to injure or ruin oneself, to cut one's own throat
(-ke), To fall at the feet (of; — see pāṅw paṛnā and pāṅw pakaṛnā)
(-ke), To fall at the feet (of), prostrate oneself; to entreat submissively, beseech, implore
v.n. To stretch the legs full length, lie comfortably or at ease, take it easy; to stretch out the legs in death, to die
(-ke), To lay hold of or seize the feet (of); to fall at the feet (of); — to beseech submissively; to prevent (one) from going
(-ke), To worship, or to kiss, the feet (of), to do honour or reverence (to); — to shun, avoid
v.n. The feet to swell; to be tired or weary; to be perplexed, or embarrassed
v.n. (or phūṅk-phūṅk-ke pāṅw rakhnā), To move cautiously or warily (in), to do anything carefully; to tread softly (as on cinders)
v.n. (A woman) to visit her parent's, or another, house, after childbirth
v.n. To sleep with the legs stretched out full length; to sleep at ease, or in peace and security; to be free from care, be perfectly contented and happy
v.n. To be full length, or at ease, &c. = pāṅw pasārnā, q.v.; — to raise one's demands; to be importunate; to be insatiable; to insist, be obstinate; to be exacting
v.n. To stamp with impatience, or anger; to struggle vainly against difficulties; to throw about the legs (in pain, anguish, or the agonies of death)
s.f. lit. 'An ant under the foot'; a poor, helpless, or miserable creature
pāṅw-tale-kī nikalnā, v.n. To feel the ground slipping, as it were, from under one's feet; to be stunned or staggered
v.n. To trample under foot, to crush; to give pain (to), to annoy
v.n. To wear out one's legs in vain; to be marched or trotted about in vain; to be tired; to desist from visiting (a person); to run vainly after (a person); to visit (one) very often
v.n. To persevere firmly (in a course, an enterprise, or a resolution)
v.n. To plant the feet firmly, to stand firmly; to get a footing or foot-hold
v.n. To have a good footing, to stand firmly
s.f. Rubbing or pressing the feet
v.n. The foot to slip, or give way; to totter, to become unstable
(-par or meṅ), To set foot (on); to come (into), step (in), enter; to land: to go or step forward, advance; to prepare (for), enter (upon), to begin

(-ke), lit. 'To drink the water in which one's feet have been washed'; to do honour or reverence (to); to place perfect confidence (in); to show respect or affection (for), to regard with the greatest respect, to look up (to)
v.n. The foot to slip or totter; to slip, to lose (one's) footing; to give way (a foundation, an army, &c.)
v.n. To rub the feet together; to go about foolishly and unprofitably; to be in the agonies of death
v.n. To be unable to stand (through excessive joy), to be unsteady on the feet (through joy)
(or, more com., zamīn-par pāṅw na rakhnā), v.n. To tread the air; to walk on stilts; to walk like a swell; to show off
v.n. The foot to be numbed, the foot to sleep
v.n. To stick close (to one), to watch (one) closely
v.n. To be near or close (to)
v.n. To occupy a fixed habitation; to adopt a new resolution
v.n. To have a tremor of the legs; to totter; to fear to attempt anything
(-ke), To convict one by his own arguments
(or, more com., gor-meṅ pāṅw laṭkānā), v.n. To have one foot in the grave; to be advanced in years
v.n. The skin of the feet to be rubbed away or worn; to be always on the tramp; to undergo useless labour
pāṅw laraznā, v.n. = pāṅw kāṅpnā, q.v.
v.n. To be always on the move
v.n. To apply henna to the feet; to be unable to set foot on the ground
v.n. To show the cloven hoof; to exceed one's proper limits; to betray one's evil disposition; to begin one's pranks; to be a ringleader (in a criminal action); to withdraw (from, -se), back out (of)
v.n. To be ready on one foot; to be ready to start (on an enterprise, &c.); to be ready and willing
v.n. To stand in the breach; to risk one's life (for another)
adv. Softly, quietly, gently; slily, stealthily, with a cat-like tread.

def: s.m. The Greek partridge ( = ćakor); — name of a Rājpūt tribe (cf. paṅwārā and paṅwāriyā).
s.m. A species of tree, Cassia tora.
s.m. = panwāṛā, q.v.

s.m. A panegyric or encomiastic piece in a kind of alliterative poetry recounting the achievements of a warrior, the talents and attainments of a scholar, or the powers, virtues and excellencies of a person generally; epic poem; ballad, legend, story, tale, fable.
s.m. A plate or dish made of leaves to eat on; — twigs of the babūl tree lopped off the trunk.
s.f. See s.v. pan, 'betel-leaf.'
s.m. A vender of pān or betel leaves.
s.m. = paṅwāṛiyā, q.v.
s.m. Panegyrist, bard, story-teller (see paṅwāṛā).
s.m. Cloth or carpet spread to walk on.
s.f. Pattens (syn. khaṛāʼoṅ); sandals.
s.f. See pāʼo-roṭī.
adj. & s.m. Saleable, vendible; — any article or commodity for sale
subs: s.f. Market, bazaar, shop; a warehouse.

def: s.m. A spaniel; a pointer.
s.f. Foot of a bedstead.

ety: Pehl. panza-dah; Zend panća-daśan; S panća-daśan
def: adj. Fifteen.
adj. Fifteenth.
s.m. Fourth part, quarter; the fourth part of a ser, a weight of about half a pound; — pāʼo ānā, s.m. One fourth of an anna, one pice
subs: adj. A ser; about a ser
pāʼo-ṭakī, s.f. The fee of a qānungo allowed by Government at the rate of four annas per hundred rupees on the net revenue of each district, to defray the expenses of the establishment
s.m. The fourth part of a circle, a quadrant
s.f. A loaf of bread made in the European way, and weighing a pāʼo or quarter of a ser, i.e. about half a pound.

def: s.m. = pāhū, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ī), A quarter of any coin; a quarter of a rupee, a four anna piece.
s.m. = pāṅw and pāṅʼoṅ, q.v.; — also (for pāṅwoṅ), 'feet.'
s.m. Name of a plant that grows in stagnant water.
v.t. = pānā, q.v.
s.f. = pāṅwṛī, q.v.
s.f. That which is gained or got; gain, income (syn. āmdanī).
s.m. Evil, sin, vice, crime, wickedness, transgression, guilt; immorality, adultery, fornication
subs: adj. & s.m.f. Of a wicked or vicious disposition, sinful, criminal, reprobate; — sinner, reprobate, wretch
adj. & s.m. Following an evil course of life, wicked, vicious; — wicked or vicious person
s.m. Pursuit of sin or vice; — pāp-āćaraṇ karnā, v.n. To lead a life of sin
v.n. To reap the reward of sins committed in a former birth
adj. Evil-minded, of corrupt intellect, sinful, ungodly, wicked, depraved, graceless
pāp kamānā, pāp mol-lenā, v.n. To commit sin, do wrong; to hug a sin; to bring calamity (upon)
adj. Bearing evil fruit, having evil consequences; inauspicious
pāp lagnā (-ko), To be stained with sin, to sin, to commit sin
s.f. The creeping plant Clypea hernandifolia
adj. & s.m. Looking at faults, censorious; — a malevolent person
adj. & s.m. Conscious of sin, knowing an act to be wicked; — one who is conscious of sin; one who is conversant with sin
s.m. The misery, or the punishment, consequent on sinning
pāp kāṭnā (-kā), To put away sin, to wipe out sin, to pardon, grant absolution or deliverance from sin
pāp kaṭnā, v.n. Sin to be blotted out, to be pardoned or absolved
s.m. Any bad disease (as leprosy, small-pox, syphilis, &c.) considered as the penalty of sin in a former life
pāp-kārī, pāp-kar, pāp-kartā, pāp kartr̤i, s.m. Sinner, criminal, guilty person
s.m. = pāp-kar, q.v.; (also) evil action, misdeed, crime
v.n. To sin; to commit adultery or fornication

vulg. pāp-girah, s.m. Inauspicious planet; any ill-omened aspect of the heavenly bodies (such as the conjunction of the sun or Budh with the moon in her last quarter); Rahu, Saturn, Mars; — calamity, misfortune, fatal destiny
adj. Destroying, effacing, or expiating sin
(-ko), To impute fault (to), to blame, charge, accuse; to calumniate
v.n. To confess sin or fault
adj. = pap-buddhi, q.v.; — pāp-matī, s.f. Sinful woman
adj. & s.m. Liberated or absolved from guilt; one who has been freed from sin or crime
vulg. pāp-moćan or pāp-moćhan, s.m. Liberation from sin, absolution, pardon, salvation — pāp mol-lenā, v.n. = pāp bisānā, q.v.

def: s.m. Weevil, an insect bred in rice ( = piṭārī).
s.m. Father ( = bābā); the Pope.
s.m. The Papaw tree, and its fruit, Carica papaya; a child's whistle ( = papīhā).
s.m. = paparmās, q.v.
s.m. Crust, thin layer; scale, scab; thin crisp cake made of any pulse (esp. of mūṅg); thin light soil, fertile soil
subs: v.n. To roll out thin crisp cakes; to undergo great hardship or suffering; to labour hard on insufficient means; to eke out a scanty livelihood.

def: s.m. Crust, thin layer; scale, scab; thin crisp cake made of any pulse (esp. of mūṅg); thin light soil, fertile soil
subs: v.n. To roll out thin crisp cakes; to undergo great hardship or suffering; to labour hard on insufficient means; to eke out a scanty livelihood.

def: s.m. Crust, &c. = pāpaṛ, q.v.
s.m. The plant Gardenia latifolia, &c. ( = pāpṛā, q.v.).
s.m. The name given in Southern India to the Pompelmose or Pumelo (in Northern India it is called ćakotrā).
adj. & s.m. Sinful, wicked, bad, guilty (of, -kā); cruel, hard-hearted; — sinner, criminal.
s.m. = papīhā, q.v.
s.m. A species of cuckoo, Cuculus melanoleneos (syn. ćāṭak); — a child's whistle ( = papaiyā).
adj. & s.m. = pāpī, q.v.
s.f. contrac. of next, q.v.
s.f. Female delinquent, wicked woman.
adj. (superl.), Most wicked; very wicked or sinful.
adj. & s.m. = pāpī, q.v.
s.m. A species of Ricinus (?); the papan ( = papaiyā, q.v.).
s.f. Eyelash (syn. palak).
v.n. To munch or mumble, to masticate (as an old man does who has no teeth; cf. polā).
s.m. Eyelid.
s.f. A kind of tree, Paretta indica(?), and its fruit (the leaves of the tree are used for poultices).
s.m. The plant Gardenia latifolia; the plantain, Musa paradisiaca; — the fruit of the ḍhāk tree (also written pappaṛ, papaṛ, papuṛā)
subs: and pāpaṛ-khār (S. parpaṭa+kshāra), s.m. Ashes of the plantain tree used instead of salt for seasoning the cakes called pāpaṛ.

def: s.m. Crust, &c. = pāpaṛ, q.v.
v.n. = papṛiyānā, q.v.
s.f. Thin crust, incrustation; scab, scale, scurf; scales formed by the drying up of the moist earth; incrustation formed on any liquid; thin, crisp cake (made of flour, &c.)
subs: v.n. To form a crust; to crystallize: papṛiy-ā-kat or kath, s.f. A kind of white-coloured kath or extract of Mimosa catechu.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Scabby, scaly, scurfy.
v.n. To form a crust or scab, &c.; to scale; to crystallize ( = papṛī jamnā).
s.m. The plant Gardenia latifolia, &c. ( = pāpṛā, q.v.).

s.m. & adv. (gov. gen. with -ke, if the postp. is expressed), Further bank, or opposite shore or side (of a river, lake, &c.); the other side, concluding bound; the end or limit of anything, termination, conclusion; utmost limit or extent or reach; bottom, innermost depth, inmost recess; first turning up or ploughing of land; — over, across, on or to the other or further side, through, beyond
subs: v.t. To put or take across, to put or ferry over; to carry through, cause to reach the end, &c. (see next)
v.n. To go or get across (esp. the ocean of existence), to cross; to reach the end; to accomplish, get through; to succeed, gain one's end
v.n. To get to the bottom (of), to fathom, to sound
v.n. To come to an end, be over

v.t. To cross over, to ferry across or over; to pierce, go through and through, transfix; to carry through, finish, accomplish, get to the end (of, -ke); to relieve; to succeed, win, gain (in gambling)

pārag, adj. & s.m. Going to the opposite shore, crossing over, going through or over or across or to the end; intending to cross; completely mastering, thoroughly well versed in, knowing, clever, skilful, expert, able; — one who has arrived at the end, &c.

part. adj. & s.m. Passed over in safety, crossed; passed beyond the world; pure, holy; — one who has reached the opposite shore; one who has passed beyond the world; (with Jains) an Arhat or deified saint or teacher

pār honā or ho-jānā, v.n. To be carried across, to reach the shore; to be completed, finished, or accomplished; to go through and through, penetrate, permeate; to get well through, be successful, to succeed; to survive; to go away, be off, escape, attain refuge
and wār-pār, adv. On both sides (of a river, &c.); from end to end, through and through, quite through.

def: adj. Past, last; next
postpn. On, upon; on the point of; up to, till; on account of, because of, in consequence of, through, for; after, according to; dependent on; notwithstanding.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀭𑀺 (pari), 𑀉𑀧𑁆𑀧𑀭𑀺 (uppari), from Sanskrit उपरि (upari, “above”).

def: conj. But; still, yet, nevertheless, however, else, otherwise, unless
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀭𑀺 (pari), 𑀉𑀧𑁆𑀧𑀭𑀺 (uppari), from Sanskrit उपरि (upari, “above”).

def: s.m. Pinion, feather, wing, quill
subs: adj. Expanding or shaking the wings: par-afshānī, s.f. Expansion or shaking of the wings
par jalnā, v.n. 'The wing to be broken,' or 'the feathers to be burnt'; to be without power or ability; to be exposed to risk or danger, to have one's wings singed, to be rendered powerless
To shake the feathers (preparatory to flying, &c.); to shed the feathers, to moult
s.f. Flapping of the wings, fluttering, flying
adj. 'Having the wing broken,' distressed, afflicted, disabled, powerless
(-ke), To clip the wings (of), to disable, cripple
adj. Having the wings clipped, wing-clipped
s.f. Feathering or winging an arrow; the feather so applied
v.n. To flap or flutter the wings, to fly; to get access to, to approach, enter
v.n. To display (one's) new feathers, to come out, to show off, to begin (one's) pranks, give oneself airs
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀭𑀺 (pari), 𑀉𑀧𑁆𑀧𑀭𑀺 (uppari), from Sanskrit उपरि (upari, “above”).

def: s.m. Scaffold, scaffolding, platform, stage, framework; wooden frame over a well.
s.m. Quicksilver, mercury; — adj. Heavy, weighty
subs: pārā pilānā (meṅ, -ko), To fill or load with quicksilver; to make heavy

def: s.m. = pahriyā, q.v.
s.m. Strap; slip, piece, bit, fragment, scrap, patch, botch
subs: s.m. Patcher, botcher; cobbler, one who applies the leather parts in tents, screens, &c.

def: s.m. (used in comp.) Quarter of a town, district, ward; cluster of huts apart from the village to which they belong, hamlet; boundary of a field.
s.m. Male buffalo-calf; calf ( = pāṭhā, q.v.
s.m. = pāṛ, pāṛā, q.v.
prep. (used at the beginning of compounds), Away, off; over, on; back, backward; inverted, reverse, sideways; wrong.
s.m. The boundary of a field ( = paḍā).
s.m. A buffalo calf ( = pāṛā, q.v.).
s.m. Scaffold, scaffolding, platform, stage, framework; wooden frame over a well.

adj. Distant, remote, removed; opposite, ulterior, beyond, further; exceeding, left or remaining, longest, ancient; other, another, strange, different, varying, foreign, alien, inimical, hostile; subsequent, succeeding, following, next, after; very high or excellent, distinguished, pre-eminent, highest, greatest, chief
subs: (para+ap˚), s.m. Laying blame on another; slander, calumny; — adj. Accusing another; slanderous

(para+adh˚), s.m. Another's office or post; — parādhikār-ćarćā, s.f. Interference with another's concerns, officiousness: parādhīn (para+ādh˚), adj. Depending on another, dependent, subject, subservient, humble

s.f. parādhīnatwa, s.m. Dependence upon another, subjection: parārth (para+artha), s.m. The highest advantage or interest; the chief meaning or importance; the highest object (euphemistic expression for sexual intercourse); the profit or interest of another; having another object or meaning, designed for another, done for another; adv. For the sake of another, for the good of another; — parārth-prārthnā, s.f. Intercession, intercessory prayer; — parārth-vādī, adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Talking of the affairs of others, officious, intermeddling, meddlesome; — a meddlesome or officious person, a busybody
s.m. A benevolent person; — also = param-ārthī, q.v.

(para+ardha), s.m. The more remote or opposite half or part, the other side or half, the ulterior part; the highest number, the number one hundred thousand billions, or (according to others) a lac of lacs of krores, or a number equal to half the term of Brahmā's life, or as many mortal days as are equal to fifty of his years; — adj. Immense, infinite

adj. & s.m. Being on the further side or half, most remote; most distant in number, of the highest possible number or value; highest in rank or quality, most highly esteemed, most costly, most excellent, most beautiful, finest, best; — an immense number, an infinite number, a maximum

(para+an˚), s.m. & adj. The food of another; food supplied by another; — eating the food of another, living at another's expense, sponging; — a servant; — parānna-bhojī, s.m. One who lives at another's expense
s.m. & adj. Dependence upon another; — dependent or relying upon another
s.f. A female dependant; a parasitical plant

(para+av˚), adj. & s.m. Far and near, remote and proximate, earlier and later, prior and subsequent, higher and lower, highest and lowest; — ancestors and descendants; the whole extent of an idea, totality; the universe
(para+āv˚), s.m. Reversal (of a sentence); — parāvartt-vyava-hār, s.m. (in law), Appeal
(para+ahna), s.m. The afternoon, the latter part of the day
(para+āy˚), adj. Dependent upon another, subject to another, subservient
s.m. The Supreme Spirit or Brahma
adj. & s.m. Cherished or nourished by a stranger, fostered, adopted; — the Indian cuckoo (supposed to leave its eggs in the nest of the crow to be natched); — nourishing another, the crow
s.m. Superior merit; good fortune, prosperity; excelcellence, supremacy; the last part, residue, remainder
s.m. An excellent protector; a benefactor
s.m. High station, eminence; final emancipation
s.m. Another man, stranger, husband of another woman; gallant, paramour
s.m. The family or dependants of another
s.m. lit. 'Fostered or nourished by a stranger,' the kokil or Indian cuckoo
s.f. A female cuckoo; a harlot, whore; a parasitical plant
s.f. A woman who has had a former husband
s.m. A second or a third hunḍī given to supply the loss of the first; duplicate of a bill of exchange
s.m. lit. 'Having recourse to the wife of another,' an adulterer
adj. Highest, greatest, most excellent, supreme; farthest
adj. Dependent upon another, dependent, subject to another, subservient, obedient
s.f. A female stranger; an adopted female
para-ja, adj. & s.m. Born of another; fostered by a stranger; — a stranger; an adopted child
par-jit, part. Conquered by another, subdued
s.m. Fault, flaw, or defect in another
s.f. Another's wife; — para-dāra-gāmī, s.m. lit. 'Going to the wife of another,' an adulterer; — para-dāra-gaman, s.m. 'Approaching the wife of another,' adultery, adulterous intercourse or intrigue
adj. Tyrannizing over another or over others, hurting or injuring others, tyrannical, oppressive
s.m. The pain or sorrow of another; — par-dukh-nivāraṇ, s.m. One who averts trouble or sorrow from another
s.m. Another's fault or crime; — para-dosh-kīrtan, s.m. The proclaiming of others' faults, censoriousness, scandal, calumny
s.m. The guilt or crime of another
s.m. Hatred or hostility towards another
adj. Hating others, hostile to another, inimical, adverse

s.m. Another's duty or business; the duties of another caste; — par-dharm, sahishṇutā, s.f. Religious toleration; — par-dharm-grāhī, adj. Adopting another religion; — par-dharm-grahaṇ, s.m. The adopting another religion, changing one's religion
s.m. Another's wealth or property; — par-dhanāswā-dhan-mukh, s.m. Feeding luxuriously at another's expense
par-deś, s.m. & adv. A foreign country; — in a foreign country, in another country, abroad
par-deśī, adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to another country, foreign, exotic; — foreigner, stranger, a new man; one residing abroad
s.f. The wife of another; an unmarried woman depending upon another (as upon her father, &c.); — par-strī-prasang, par-strī-grahaṇ, par-strī-gaman, s.m. Criminal intercourse with another's wife
adj. & s.m. Approaching the wife of another; an adulterer
s.m. Relation to or connection with another
adj. Related to another; belonging to another
s.m. The welfare of another; beneficence, benevolence
adj. & s.m. Benevolent; — a benevolent person
par-kārya, s.m. Another's business or interest, the affairs or concerns of another; — par-kāj-meṅ hāth ḍālnā, To intermeddle in the affairs of another
adj. & s.m. Attentive to the business, concerns, or interests of others; serving others; — a beneficent person; a busybody
s.m. Another's field; another's body; another's wife

s.m. The other world, the next world, the future state; the heavenly paradise; — par-lok sudhārnā, v.n. To prepare, or pave the way, for the next world by good deeds ( = āḵẖirat saṅwārnā); — par-lok-gāmī, adj. Going to the other world, dying; — par-lok-gam, par-lok gaman, s.m. Going to the next world, dying, death
s.m. Another's opinion, different opinion or way of thinking; different doctrine, heterodoxy; — credit, esteem, reputation, name
s.m. One who depends upon the opinion or advice of another
s.f. Another woman; another's wife ( = par-strī)
s.f. Reviling others, abuse or ridicule of others; slander, calumny
(para+upadeś), s.m. Giving advice to others, counselling others; admonishing, exhorting or instructing another, instruction of others
paropkār (para+upakār), s.m. The assisting others, doing good to another, beneficence, benevolence, charity
s.m. Readiness to help or to do good to others, beneficence, kindness, humanity
paropkārak = paropkārī, adj. & s.m. Assisting others, supporting others; kind to others, beneficent, charitable, philanthropic; — a kind-hearted or beneficent person, a philanthropist

par-vaś, vulg. par-bas, adj. & s.m. Subject to another, depending on the will of another, dependent, subservient, subject; precarious; deceived, wheedled, inveigled; — dependence, subjection: par-vaśyatā, s.f. or par-vaśyatva, s.m. Dependence on another, subjection.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀭𑀺 (pari), 𑀉𑀧𑁆𑀧𑀭𑀺 (uppari), from Sanskrit उपरि (upari, “above”).

def: part. adj. Laid aside; lying (unused, unowned, unemployed, or unoccupied); useless, idle; prostrate; uncultivated, fallow (land); — adv. In its place; as it is
subs: v.t. To find lying; to find; to get easily or for nothing
v.n. To wander about; to idle
or paṛe rahnā, v.n. To remain lying; to loll or sleep all day long, to be idle; to be laid on one's back, be prostrated (through sickness or grief); to be helpless, poor, or destitute
s.m. (in Geom.) A surface; a horizontal plane
(-par, or -pe), To hang or depend (upon), to sponge (on).

def: s.m. A body or line (of troops), rank, file, company, troop; a flock, herd (of animals)
subs: v.n. To form line; to draw up in line of battle (see پره para).

def: s.m. A gem, a flat diamond.
s.m. Disappearance; overcoming; defeat, discomfiture; mortification, humiliation, disgrace.
part. Overcome, vanquished, defeated, discomfited.
s.m. A fine or penance imposed on religious occasions as the price of absolution, expiation, atonement.
v.t. To move a lamp or candle over the heads of a bride and bridegroom (in order to drive away evil spirits, propitiate good ones, &c.); to kindle (a fire), light (a pipe, &c. = parćānā).
s.m. A fine or penance imposed on religious occasions as the price of absolution, expiation, atonement.

v.n. To be made acquainted (with), be introduced (to), to become acquainted (with); to be conversant or familiar (with); to become intimate or familiar; to be tamed or domesticated; to be accustomed (to), be habituated (to).
s.m. Quicksilver = pārā, q.v.
s.m. A horse of the Persian breed.
s.f. See s.v. para, par.
s.m. Adulterer.
s.m. Adultery.

r: pāra-deśī, pār-deśī, pāra-deśik, pār-deśik, pāra-deśya, pār-deśya
def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to another or foreign country, &c. = pardesī, q.v.

r: pāra-deśik, pār-deśik, pāra-deśī, pār-deśī, pāra-deśya, pār-deśya
def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to another or foreign country, &c. = pardesī, q.v.

r: pāra-deśya, pār-deśya, pāra-deśik, pār-deśik, pāra-deśī, pār-deśī
def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to another or foreign country, &c. = pardesī, q.v.
See s.v. para, par, for this and other words beginning with par that are not found below.
s.m. The pollen or farina of a flower; dust; fragrant powder used after bathing; sandal; an eclipse of the sun or moon; name of a mountain.
s.m. (Poet.) = parāg, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = pārak, q.v. See also s.v. pāra, pār.

ety: perf. part. of parā-gandan; Zend pairi+rt. kan = S. pari+khan
def: part. adj. Widely separated or parted, widely sundered, scattered, dispersed, dissipated, routed; distracted, disturbed (in mind)
subs: (-se), To be widely separated (from); to be scattered, dispersed, &c.

def: s.f. Separation, dispersion.
part. adj. & s.m. See s.v. pār.
s.f. Fleeing, running away; flight, escape; a general emigration.
s.m. = S پراهنه परान्ह parāhna, s.m. See s.v. para, par.
adj. See s.v. para, par.
s.m. = S پراه पराह parāh, s.m. See s.v. para, par.

ety: irreg. pl., after the Ar., of pārća
def: s.m. Shreds, bits of cloth, &c.
s.f. Hilt (of a sword); handle (of a shield); — name of a rāginī or musical mode
s.m. Loss, defeat, discomfiture, overthrow; victory, conquest; being cast in a law-suit
subs: adj. & s.m. Overcoming, surpassing, defeating; overcome, defeated; — one who has been defeated, one who has lost a law-suit; one who has conquered, conqueror, winner.

def: adj. & s.m. = parājay-mān, q.v.s.v. parājay.
part. adj. Suffering a defeat, losing a suit, defeated, overcome, conquered.
part. See s.v. para.
adj. & s.m. Purifying; protecting, cherishing; pleasing; — enabling (one) to cross a river, or to get through the world; getting through, accomplishing.
adj. Completely familiar or conversant with, knowing thoroughly, well versed in; skilful, expert, clever, able.

s.m. A sort of penance or religious vow of an expiatory kind (said to consist in fasting for twelve days and nights and keeping the mind attentive and the organs subdued); a sacrificial sword or scimitar.
s.f. Inspection, examination, test, trial, proof, experiment; scrutiny; discrimination, judgment.
s.m. Examiner, tester, discriminator, judge, critic, connoiseur.
s.m. One who examines and approves money, money-tester, assayer, banker; tester, inspector, examiner, prover.
v.t. To get, obtain, secure.
v.t. To examine, prove, try, test, assay
s.f. = parkhāʼī, q.v.
v.t. = parkhānā, q.v.
s.f. The mistress or wife or another.
adj. Belonging to another (see parakiyā).
v.t. = parakhnā, q.v.
v.n. To become habituated or accustomed (to), to acquire a habit.
s.m. Power, vigour, strength, ability, exertion, valour, prowess, courage, spirit
subs: parākram-vant, adj. Possessed of power or strength, endowed with courage, courageous, spirited, valourous, heroic.

def: adj. Spirited, bold, courageous, showing or displaying courage or strength, exerting power; strong, stout, vigorous, powerful.
s.m. See s.v. para, par.
s.m. The tree Stereospermum suaveolens, or Bignonia suaveolens; also S. (or B.) chelonoides.
s.f. Straw ( = puwāl, puʼāl; bićālī).
adj. Relating to the next world.

adj. Highest, first, most excellent, most distinguished, best, greatest, chief, primary, principal, superior, paramount, supreme; transcendent, perfect; exceeding, excessive, extreme, very great; — adv. Exceedingly, extremely, very much, in the highest degree, to a great degree, excellently
subs: (parama+r̤ishi), s.m. 'Greatest sage,' a r̤ishi or divine sage of a peculiar order or division
(˚ma+ābh˚), s.m. An excellent ornament; splendid attire
vulg. parmātmā (˚ma+āt˚), s.m. The Supreme Spirit, soul of the universe; — paramātmā-purush, s.m. Idem
(˚ma+ad˚), s.m. 'The highest without a duplicate,' an epithet of Vishnu; pure unitarianism

(˚ma+ar˚), s.m. The highest or most sublime truth, the whole truth; real truth; spiritual knowledge; the greatest good; the best sense; any excellent or important aim or object, the first pursuit, the best end; virtue, merit; — paramārth sudharnā, v.n. To arrange or provide for the highest good or the best end, to do good deeds in order to secure salvation; — paramārth-ke hetu lagānā, To give alms with a view to salvation; — paramārth-vādī, s.m. One who teaches the most sublime truth, &c.
adj. & s.m. Pursuing the highest purpose, &c.; — a religious or virtuous person
(˚ma+āg˚), s.f. 'The supreme pathway,' the proceeding to heaven and final beatitude
(˚ma+an˚), s.m. 'Best food,' rice boiled in milk with sugar (offered to gods or to the manes)

(˚ma+aṇu˚), s.m. An infinitesimal particle, an atom, the invisible base of all aggregate bodies (of which thirty are said to form a mote in a sunbeam); the sun's passage past an atom of matter, an infinitesimal division of time; the lowest measure of weight
s.f. Infinite minuteness, the state of an atom
(˚ma+ān˚), s.m. 'Having a large face,' the Sun
(˚ma+ān˚), s.m. Supreme felicity, great pleasure, high delight; crowning joy, final beatitude; the Supreme Spirit
(˚ma+āy˚), s.m.f. The longest period of life (in men or brutes)
s.m. A chief devotee; a fervent devotee, a very pious person
s.m. An epithet of God, or of any river or deity by whom spiritual purification is effected
s.m. The highest rank, high station; excellence; final beatitude
s.m. The chief thing, final salvation (the attainment of emancipation from the necessity of farther transmigration)
s.m. The highest degree of splendour or effulgence
adj. Possessing the highest degree of splendour or glory
adj. 'Blessed in the highest sense,' an epithet of the ever-blessed God
param-gat, s.f. The highest attainment or condition, final beatitude; any chief object or refuge (as a god, or a protector)
(prop. param-jṅāna), s.m. The highest wisdom; knowledge of the Highest
s.m. The highest happiness, salvation; — param-mukh-dāyak, adj. & s.m. Bestowing the highest happiness or salvation; — one who bestows the highest happiness, &c.
s.m. Complete destruction, utter ruin; the ruin of one's highest interests
s.f. 'The chief of courtesans,' an epithet of Rādhā
s.m. An ascetic of the highest order, a religious man who has subdued all his senses by abstract meditation
parmeś = parameśvar, parmeśvar (˚ma+īś˚), s.m. Supreme lord (said of rich or illustrious men, of princes, and of gods); the Supreme Lord; an epithet of Vishnu
parameśā, s.f. 'The supreme goddess,' 'the consort of the supreme lord Śiva,' the goddess Durgā or Devī
parmeśvarīya, adj. Of or pertaining to the Supreme Being, &c.
parmeshṭī (S. parame+shṭhī), adj. & s.m. Standing at the head or top, highest, chief, principal; — the Supreme Being; any supreme deity.

def: s.m. Remembering, recollection; reflection, consideration, thought; discrimination, judgment; advice, counsel; consultation
adj. & s.m. Calling or bringing to mind, reminding; — discriminator; adviser, counsellor.
adj. & s.m. Calling or bringing to mind, reminding; — discriminator; adviser, counsellor.
see s.v. param for compounds which have this word for the first member.
adj. Relating to a high or spiritual object, relating to supreme truth; of the highest importance; most desirable, preferable, best, excellent, superior; real, essential, true.

ety: S. प्रामोदनं, rt. मुद् with प्र
def: s.m. Pleasing, coaxing, wheedling.

ety: S. प्रामोदनं, rt. मुद् with प्र
def: s.m. Pleasing, coaxing, wheedling.

adv. & s.m. One following the other; proceeding from one to another (as from father to son, &c.); successive, repeated; — one after the other, successively, in continuous succession; — a great-great-grandson.

s.f. An uninterrupted series, row, regular series, succession, continuous arrangement, continuance, order, method; tradition; impression; race, progeny, lineage; a great-great-granddaughter; — paramparā, or paramparā-se, adv. Successively, in continuous succession; in lineal succession, from father to son; from time immemorial.
s.f. Tradition ( = paramparā).
adj. Hereditary, obtained by inheritance or descent; traditional.

adj. & s.m. Passing on from one to another; one following the other; uninterrupted series, continuous order, hereditary succession, hereditary property; traditional instruction or knowledge, tradition
s.m. Fulfilling, accomplishing; reading through, reading, studying; — concluding a fast, eating or drinking after a fast, breakfast; one who eats after a fast; — a cloud
s.f. A very quick time in music.
v.n. To run, to flee.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀼𑀭𑀸𑀡 (purāṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit पुराण (purāṇa).
